summary: Typosquat of metamask-sdk exfiltrates SSH keys,.env files, and wallet/cloud credentials at install time
details: @wagni_bot/metemask-sdk is a single-character typosquat of the legitimate metamask-sdk (self-described as 'Unofficial metemask-sdk SDK') whose postinstall.js runs a credential stealer on npm install. The script walks the current working directory, $HOME, and ~/.ssh for files whose names contain.pem,.key, id_rsa, id_ed25519,.cred, or.env, reads their contents with fs.readFileSync, and enumerates process.env filtering for keys containing PRIVATE, SECRET, TOKEN, API_KEY, PASSWORD, MNEMONIC, SEED, WALLET, or AWS. It gathers host identity via os.hostname() and os.userInfo() and POSTs the collected file contents, environment variables, and host metadata over plain HTTP to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at http://107.161.90.180:7777. This is unambiguous install-time exfiltration of installer-owned SSH private keys, environment credentials, wallet mnemonics/seeds, and cloud access keys, targeted at developers who mistype 'metamask'.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
